codeReview.summary: Rate-limiting middleware and tests are implemented, but the WebSocket runtime is not wired to live indexed state/events, so required real-time channel behavior is not delivered in actual service execution.

issues.description: WS process creates its own fresh OrderbookState and does not start/use EventIndexer or any bridge to runtime event streams. As a result, orderbook/trades/account channels do not receive live updates from order placement/fills/balance changes, so expected behavior ('delta updates on order placement', 'trades channel events', 'account:{address} balance updates') is not actually implemented outside unit tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker skill requires tests-first (TDD) and manual endpoint verification, but transcript shows implementation files were created before new tests and no manual runtime verification commands (e.g., wscat/curl against running services) were executed; handoff still marks followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton: Create api/src/websocket/ws-server.ts and api/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts occurred before Create api/src/websocket/ws-server.test.ts and api/src/middleware/rate-limit.test.ts; no command like 'pnpm dev:ws' + wscat/curl appears.

summary: Feature is marked fail due to missing runtime integration between WebSocket server and indexed order/trade/balance event flow. REST rate limiting appears correct with good test coverage, but core WS expected behaviors are only validated via direct test-time broadcast calls, not actual service wiring.
